page: Microsoft Azure D2ls_v6 is a low-memory, compute-oriented virtual machine from the Dlsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical Intel Xeon 8573B core with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz, this x86_64 architecture processor includes an L3 cache of 260 MB and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions) for tile and int8 operations. The instance is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Performance benchmarks reveal a strong single-core profile but limited multi-core scaling due to the 2-vCPU configuration. The instance achieves a strong Geekbench single-core score of 2134.0 and a strong single-threaded gzip compression rate of 31.36 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory-intensive benchmarks are weaker. The Geekbench multi-core score is 2620.0, and the stress-ng multi-core performance is 1984.78. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with uncached read speeds at 20.87 GB/sec and write speeds at 14.17 GB/sec. Database operations and static web serving throughput are relatively low, with Redis SET operations reaching 2.78 million operations per second and web serving throughput at 4.51 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D2ls_v6 offers a low-cost entry point for workloads requiring high clock-speed single-threaded performance without massive memory footprints. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs keeps resource density focused on the CPU. This virtual machine is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, small-scale text processing, and low-intensity utility workloads. It is not recommended for memory-intensive databases, heavy multi-threaded compilation, or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D2ls_v6 is a low-memory, compute-oriented virtual machine from the Dlsv6 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573B processor. It features 2 vCPUs (1 physical core with hyper-threading) and 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks bundled local storage and GPUs but supports premium storage. Benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2134.0 and fast single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are limited, as evidenced by a weak Geekbench multi-core score of 2620.0 and uncached read speeds of 20.87 GB/sec. This server is qualitatively cost-effective for lightweight, single-threaded tasks, small-scale text processing, and basic utility workloads that do not require large memory allocations or high multi-threaded performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2ls_v6 is a 2-vCPU virtual machine with 4.0 GB RAM, featuring a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It offers strong single-core performance for lightweight, compute-focused workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2ls_v6 is a 2-vCPU, 4.0 GB RAM virtual machine featuring a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor for lightweight compute workloads.
tagline: An efficient low memory virtual machine featuring high clock speed processors designed for single threaded workloads and basic utility tasks.
bullet_points: Features 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573B processor
Delivers strong single-core performance with a Geekbench single-core score of 2134.0
Lacks bundled local storage and GPUs, prioritizing low-memory compute efficiency
Supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and utility tasks
